name: Ryazan Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the Russian Orthodox tradition and the snowy plains of Ryazan
wealth, faith and the harvest of the Ryazan agricultural lands
the courage shed by the people of Ryazan in defence of their land

description: Three horizontal bands of white (top), yellow (centre, double-width) and red (bottom), with the figure of the Ryazan prince holding a sword centred on the yellow band.
history: Adopted on 12 November 1997 by Law No. 60-OZ of the Ryazan Oblast Duma; the figure is taken from the historic Ryazan coat of arms granted in 1779.

features.symbolism: Three horizontal bands of white, golden yellow and red, with the historical Ryazan prince figure in the centre
the standing figure of a Russian prince in princely garb holding a sword and the scabbard, the historic emblem of Ryazan; the figure is sometimes interpreted as the Grand Prince Oleg of Ryazan
